frame_analyses.description: Initial view showing training board with marked targets and yellow Penrose drain. Laparoscopic instruments are positioned for needle loading.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean setup with clear visualization of marked targets on foam board

frame_analyses.description: Needle being introduced through tissue in supinating motion as indicated by overlay text. C-loop formation beginning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle orientation and controlled tissue penetration

frame_analyses.description: C-loop formation continues with proper instrument positioning. Suture being passed around instruments for first throw construction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Systematic approach to loop formation with good instrument coordination

frame_analyses.description: Square knot formation in progress. Both instruments engaged with suture material, creating crossing pattern for first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper crossing technique visible, appears to be constructing surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: First throw being tightened with both instruments applying tension. Square knot configuration maintained.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tensioning technique with instruments maintaining proper angles

frame_analyses.description: Slip knot formation for second throw. Right instrument creating loop while left maintains tension on first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean transition to second throw with appropriate hand positioning

frame_analyses.description: Slide and lock phase - second throw being secured with proper instrument positioning and suture manipulation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth execution of second throw with good control

frame_analyses.description: Final throw construction with instruments positioned for knot completion. Suture ends being properly managed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Methodical approach to final throw with maintained tension

frame_analyses.description: Final knot appears secure with good approximation. Both suture ends visible and instruments ready for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completed knot shows good security and tissue approximation

knot_assessments.notes: Well-constructed surgeon's knot with double wrap visible
Proper hand alternation with clean single throw
Final throw completes secure knot with good tissue approximation

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Excellent tissue approximation with no visible gaps in the Penrose drain closure

technique_summary: Excellent intracorporeal knot tying demonstration with proper surgeon's knot on first throw, appropriate hand alternation, and secure final result. Clean execution throughout all phases.
improvement_suggestions: Slightly more precise suture placement to minimize deviation from marked targets
Could potentially reduce time in first throw phase with more efficient instrument movements
strengths: Proper surgeon's knot construction on first throw
Secure final knot with good tissue approximation
